title: NBA Offseason Daily Digest
subtitle: July 23, 2026 – Free Agency Frenzy & Summer League Wrap-Up

items.headline: LeBron James Reportedly 'Irritated' by Adam Silver Amid Free Agency Stalemate
items.summary: LeBron James is reportedly 'irritated' by NBA Commissioner Adam Silver's public pressure to finalize his free agency decision. James' camp was allegedly ready to announce last week but decided to hold off, with Silver citing James' choice as a factor delaying the 2026-27 schedule release.

items.headline: Mavs Retain Moussa Cisse, Match Knicks' Offer Sheet
items.summary: The Dallas Mavericks have officially re-signed restricted free agent center Moussa Cisse today, matching an offer sheet from the New York Knicks for a reported $4.7 million over two years. Cisse, who tied a Mavericks rookie record with 20 rebounds last season, provides key depth in the paint.

items.headline: Hawks Acquire Lu Dort in Three-Team Deal, Mavs Land Zaccharie Risacher
items.summary: The Atlanta Hawks have officially acquired guard Lu Dort and Ryan Nembhard in a three-team trade involving the OKC Thunder and Dallas Mavericks. The Mavericks, in turn, secured forward Zaccharie Risacher, while the Thunder received three future second-round picks.

items.headline: Lakers Send Deandre Ayton to Wizards in Frontcourt Reshuffle
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have traded center Deandre Ayton to the Washington Wizards as part of their post-LeBron frontcourt adjustments. In return, the Lakers received guard Jaden Hardy and second-round picks in 2031 and 2032.

items.headline: Blockbuster Trade Sends Giannis Antetokounmpo to Miami Heat
items.summary: In one of the offseason's biggest moves, Giannis Antetokounmpo has been traded to the Miami Heat. The Milwaukee Bucks received guard Tyler Herro, center Kel'el Ware, forward Jaime Jaquez Jr., guard Kasparas Jakučionis, the No. 13 pick in the 2026 Draft, 2031 and 2033 first-round picks, and a 2030 pick swap.

items.headline: D'Angelo Russell's Memphis Tenure Potentially Short-Lived
items.summary: Acquired by the Memphis Grizzlies on July 8 as part of a complex six-team trade, D'Angelo Russell's future with the team is already uncertain. Reports indicate the Grizzlies are exploring a new trade or a contract buyout, as he may not fit their long-term rebuilding plans around their 2026 No. 3 pick Cameron Boozer.

items.headline: LeBron James' Decision Continues to Shape Free Agency Landscape
items.summary: The NBA free agent market remains in a holding pattern as teams await LeBron James' decision. With suitors like the Heat, Cavs, 76ers, Timberwolves, and Warriors, his choice is crucial, even impacting the upcoming season's schedule release.

items.headline: Mario Hezonja Drawing NBA Interest for Potential Return
items.summary: After starring for Real Madrid, former No. 5 overall pick Mario Hezonja is reportedly drawing interest from multiple NBA teams, including some pursuing LeBron James, for a potential return to the league.

items.headline: Bradley Beal Among Players Awaiting LeBron's Free Agency Call
items.summary: Bradley Beal, who declined a $5.6 million player option with the Clippers, is reportedly in a holding pattern alongside other free agents like DeMar DeRozan. Interest from teams such as the Heat, Celtics, and Clippers is expected to pick up once LeBron James makes his decision.

items.headline: Golden State Warriors Crowned 2026 NBA Summer League Champions
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors captured the 2026 NBA Summer League title, defeating the Memphis Grizzlies 94-90 in a thrilling final on July 19 in Las Vegas. Rookie Yaxel Lendeborg was named the final's Most Valuable Player, leading the Warriors' comeback with 21 points and 10 rebounds.

items.headline: Houston Rockets Add 'Shot Doctor' Chip Engelland to Coaching Staff
items.summary: The Houston Rockets have bolstered Ime Udoka's coaching staff by hiring longtime NBA development coach and renowned 'shot doctor' Chip Engelland, known for his player development and shooting expertise.

items.headline: Wemby, Clark, and Rose Grace NBA 2K27 Covers
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs' Victor Wembanyama, Indiana Fever's Caitlin Clark, and former NBA MVP Derrick Rose have been revealed as the cover athletes for NBA 2K27. Wembanyama will appear on the Standard Edition, Clark on the Deluxe, and Rose on the limited-time Ultra Edition.
